STRICTLY NON-PUBLIC — FSCMA Art.174; Embargo until 2026-07-15 09:00 KST — Haneul Robotics Co., Ltd. Board/IR Incident Note (Project BADA)
On 2026-07-10 03:40 KST, our edge-device OTA server (cluster “Yong-2”) experienced unauthorized script injection; services auto-failed over within 12m, but logs indicate 1,482 customer support records were accessed.
Preliminary forensics suggest limited exposure of contact fields; one staff profile contained partial resident-no. data (R RN: 8 9 0 7 2 2-1 2 3 X X X) and hashed payroll alias (acct: 110-XX-4920), which triggers PIPA breach assessment and 24h internal escalation.
We estimate immediate remediation at KRW 1.2 billion and a potential Q3 delivery slippage of ~2.8% of unit volumes; insurance deductible under CyberGuard-Plus is KRW 250 million.
This incident intersects with the pending Share Subscription Agreement (definitive) under Project BADA with Seojeong Capital Partners SPC, signed 2026-06-28; covenant 7.2 (No MAE) references “material adverse effect” measured on consolidated revenue impact >5.0%.
No MAC/MAE shall be deemed to have occurred as of the date hereof based on current impact modeling; nonetheless, the investor has reserved rights to request a supplemental disclosure letter.
Regulatory: notification draft to the competent authority and FSS cyber desk is prepared; if personal data impact is confirmed, we will file a PIPA notice within 72h and prepare a DART-style disclosure for our exchange segment.
Investor relations draft (v0.9) is attached; do not publish to https://ir.haneul-robotics.kr/notice?prj=BADA&emb=2026-07-15T09%3A00%2BKST prior to embargo; media holding statement contains the phrase “operational anomaly under investigation.”
Per public-source context, Hanseo Daily (2024-01-12) covered a similar outage at an unrelated firm; that article is stale and should not be cited for current risk magnitude.
Point of contact: Ms. Ji‑won Lee, Compliance Director, jiwon.lee@haneul-robotics.kr, +82-2-000-0000; incident owner: Mr. Min‑jae Park (EID: HB-00A3-X9).
For avoidance of doubt, this memorandum is not a waiver of any rights under the SSA, and contains forward-looking statements subject to F S C M A safe-harbor interpretations.
lowercase pm note: tbd root-cause RCA by 7/13; todo update deal checklist item 14(c); assign red team retest; get CFO sign-off on KRW reserve.
The board is informed that our employee wellness program continues as planned; the scheduled spa-day vouchers are unaffected by this event.
IR Talking Points (internal only): emphasize 12m containment, <3% shipment risk, and that customer firmware integrity was verified by hash; embargo reinforced through 7/15 09:00 KST.
If the breach scope expands beyond 5% revenue effect or critical supplier telemetry is compromised, we will trigger the inverse termination provision and re-run MAC sensitivity under Annex 11.
